topic.name: Federal Student Loan Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various amendments and acts related to the Higher Education Act of 1965 that aim to modify federal student loan programs, repayment options, and borrower protections.

Document 1: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to extend by one year the Secretary of Education's authority to purchase Federal Family Education Loans (FFELs).
Extends by one year the authority of institutions of higher education to participate in the FFEL lender-of-last-resort program.
Document 2: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to replace several existing federal student loan programs with a single repayment plan that caps annual debt repayments based on the borrower's income.
Establishes the Income Dependent Education Assistance (IDEA) Loan Program and the IDEA Loan Repayment Program.
Document 3: Amends title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to allow borrowers of Federal Direct Stafford Loans to extend the repayment period under certain conditions.
Subsidizes the interest that accrues on Direct Stafford Loans for the first six months of that extended grace period.
Document 4: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to make changes to the formula for calculation of individual Pell Grants.
Amends the Federal Family Education Loan program to lower the percentage of a borrower's student loan payment made subsequent to default.
Document 5: Establishes a part I Federal Supplemental Loan program under the Higher Education Act of 1965.
Requires the Secretary of Education to offer Supplemental loan borrowers the same repayment plans offered under the Direct Loan program.
Document 6: Amends the federal bankruptcy code to permit a borrower to discharge in bankruptcy a nonprofit, government, or private student loan.
Prohibits collecting the amount owed on a defaulted federal student loan through wage garnishment.
Document 7: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require proprietary institutions of higher education to spend a certain percentage of revenue on educational expenses.
Directs the Secretary of Education to define 'educational and related expenses'.
Document 8: Amends title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to direct the Secretary of Education to establish a program to refinance unpaid principal and interest on Federal Direct Loans.
Sets the interest rate on the refinanced loans based on specific criteria.
Document 9: Amends title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to reduce the interest rates on Federal Direct Unsubsidized Loans and Federal Direct PLUS Loans.
Document 10: Amends title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to modify federal student aid eligibility criteria.
Repeals a provision that suspends federal student aid eligibility following drug offenses.

topic.name: Voting and Employment Rights Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislation related to voting rights, election integrity, and employment protections for servicemembers and workers.

Document 1: Servicemember Employment Protection Act of 2012
Protects under USERRA an individual who is absent from employment
Document 2: Workforce Democracy and Fairness Act
Amends the National Labor Relations Act to revise requirements for determination by the National Labor Relation Board
Document 3: Federal Election Integrity Act of 2010
Makes it unlawful for a chief state election administration official to take active part in political management
Document 4: Voter Confidence and Increased Accessibility Act of 2007
Requires the voting system to require the use of or produce an individual, durable, voter-verified paper ballot
Document 5: Vote Integrity and Verification Act of 2007
Amends the Help America Vote Act of 2002 with respect to ballot verification and mandatory paper record audit capacity
Document 6: Workforce Democracy and Fairness Act
Requires the NLRB to require that no investigative hearing take place until at least 14 days after the filing of an election petition
Document 7: National Advisory Voter Initiative Act of 2010
Grants the people the right to propose any law which the Congress has authority to pass
Document 8: Fair Representation in Elections Act of 2011
Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the NLRB to decide, before the election of a labor organization
Document 9: Amends the Uniformed and Overseas Citizens Absentee Voting Act
Permits a valid voter registration and absentee ballot request submitted by an absent U.S. military or civilian overseas voter
Document 10: Weekend Voting Act
Establishes the first Saturday and Sunday after the first Friday in November as the days for the election

topic.name: Electric Transportation and Energy Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at advancing electric transportation technologies and promoting clean energy initiatives in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Energy Independence and Security Act of 2007 to direct the Secretary of Energy to make loans as well as grants for qualified electric transportation projects.
Directs the Secretary to inventory existing electric transportation technologies and hybrid transportation technologies.
Document 2: Direct the Secretary of Commerce to establish a program to provide grants to states to establish revolving loan funds to provide loans to small and medium-sized manufacturers to finance the cost of reequipping, expanding, or establishing manufacturing facilities in the United States to produce clean energy technology.
Includes as an activity of Regional Centers for the Transfer of Manufacturing Technology the establishment of a clean energy manufacturing supply chain initiative.
Document 3: Amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to include projects for infrastructure for provision and distribution of alternative fuels among the types of innovative technology projects that are eligible for loan guarantees.
Directs the Secretary to develop a methodology for calculating the equivalent volumes of conventional fuel displaced by use of each alternative fuel.

topic.name: Medicare and Medicaid Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at improving and regulating Medicare and Medicaid programs, focusing on healthcare access, fraud prevention, and integrated care models.

Document 1: Seniors and Taxpayers Obligation Protection Act of 2008 - Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish and implement procedures to change the Medicare beneficiary identifier
Requires the Secretary to establish a system to identify the 50 counties most vulnerable to Medicare fraud.
Document 2: Dialysis PATIENT Demonstration Act of 2016 or the Patient Access to Integrated-care, Empowerment, Nephrologists and Treatment Demonstration Act of 2016
This bill amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to establish a demonstration program for the provision of integrated care to Medicare beneficiaries with end-stage renal disease.
Document 3: Medicare Long-Term Care Hospital Improvement Act of 2009 - Amends the Medicare, Medicaid, and SCHIP Extension Act of 2007
Establishes a formula for annual adjustment of the base prospective payment rate for LTCHs for rate years 2011 through 2015.
Document 4: Rural Hospital and Provider Equity Act of 2012 - Expresses the sense of the Senate that residents of rural and frontier communities should have access to affordable, quality health care
Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act with respect to the Medicare disproportionate share hospital (DSH) adjustment for rural hospitals.
Document 5: Medicare Durable Medical Equipment Access Act of 2007 - Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to prohibit the Secretary of Health and Human Services from awarding any contracts under the competitive durable medical equipment items and services acquisition program unless
Modifies requirements for the protection of small suppliers in bidding and contracting.
Document 6: CHIP Extension and Improvement Act of 2015 - Revises and extends through FY2019 at generally increased levels the program under title XXI (State Children's Health Insurance) (CHIP)
Makes permanent the express lane option and the CHIP outreach and enrollment grant program.
Document 7: Federal and State Insurance Exchange Access Fairness and Penalty Delay Act of 2013 - Amends the Internal Revenue Code to delay the effective date of the penalty for failure to maintain minimum essential health insurance coverage
Requires the Inspector General to consider monthly reports by the Comptroller General on the progress of the websites.
Document 8: Medicare and Medicaid Improvements and Adjustments Act of 2015 - This bill amends titles XVIII (Medicare) and XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to modify payment rules and other provisions related to the Medicare and Medicaid programs
The bill expands criminal penalties and civil monetary penalties for certain acts involving federal health care programs.
Document 9: Comprehensive Immunosuppressive Drug Coverage for Kidney Transplant Patients Act of 2009 - Amends SSA title II to continue entitlement to prescription drugs used in immunosuppressive therapy furnished to an individual who receives a kidney transplant
Applies deductible and coinsurance requirements to the provision of such drugs.
Document 10: Medicaid Long-Term Care Reform Act of 2012 - Expresses the sense of Congress that Congress should repeal the Community Living Assistance Services and Supports Act
Directs the Secretary to provide to states technical assistance on the implementation and administration of qualified state LTC insurance partnerships.

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at providing tax relief and benefits to individuals and families.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to increase to $7,500 the amount of employer-provided dependent care assistance that an employee may exclude from gross income.
Allows an annual inflation adjustment to such increased amount after 2012.
Document 2: Makes permanent provisions of the Economic Growth and Tax Relief Reconciliation Act of 2001 that eliminate the marriage penalty in the standard deduction.
Eliminates the marriage penalty in the earned income tax credit.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow amounts in a flexible spending arrangement (FSA) that are not spent for medical care to be distributed to the FSA participant as taxable income.
Includes such FSAs in the definition of tax-exempt cafeteria plans.
Document 4: Extends the period in which death gratuities may be contributed to a Roth IRA from one to three years after receipt.
Amends the Internal Revenue Code to benefit survivors of members of the Armed Forces.
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend until January 1, 2012, the exemption from payment of employment taxes.
Targets individuals who begin employment after December 31, 2010.
Document 6: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make residents of Puerto Rico eligible for the earned income tax credit.
Addresses tax relief for residents of Puerto Rico.
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow certain employers a business-related tax credit for wages paid during family and medical leave.
Limits the allowable amount of such credit to $4,000 per employee.
Document 8: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to repeal withholding of tax requirements for income, railroad retirement, and social security taxes.
Requires estimated tax payments for social security and railroad retirement taxes.
Document 9: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to waive the minimum distribution requirements for certain defined contribution pension plans during calendar year 2016.
Targets retirement plan regulations.
Document 10: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to waive minimum distribution requirements for tax-exempt retirement plans for calendar years 2013 and 2014.

topic.name: Consumer Protection Legislation
